name: DarkIris Inc.

profile.description: DarkIris Inc. is a comprehensive technology enterprise engaged in the development, publishing, and operating of mobile digital games on various platforms in Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan. Founded in 2017 and headquartered in Sheung Wan, Hong Kong, the company is expanding into AI-Generated Content (AIGC) for gaming and film production through its Singapore-based AETHER INTELLIGENCE R&D subsidiary. The company completed a 1-for-16 reverse stock split in May 2026 and has a market capitalization of approximately $5.8 million.

innovation.label: WEAK
innovation.analysis: Track Record (0/3): DarkIris remains a micro-cap mobile game developer with no significant product launches or business model transformations in the last five years. The company publishes mobile games in Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan but has not launched any breakout titles or expanded meaningfully into new markets [1][2]. AI Deployment (0/2): The company announced an AIGC platform initiative in its 2026 Chairman's Letter and established AETHER INTELLIGENCE PTE. LTD. in Singapore as a global R&D hub in April 2026, but the platform remains in alpha/beta testing with no products shipping and no measurable AI-driven cost savings or revenue reported [3][4]. Market Share (0/2): DarkIris is a tiny participant in the mobile gaming market with approximately $10 million in annual revenue, no recognizable IP franchises, and no evidence of market share leadership in any category [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): The AIGC platform has a specific Beta 1.0 release target for Q3 2026 and the company has outlined a Model-as-a-Service and Platform-as-a-Service roadmap, though revenue visibility remains speculative [3][4]. R&D Intensity (0/1): With only 19 employees and limited capital resources, R&D spending is minimal in absolute terms despite the company's stated ambitions in AI [2].

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (1/4): DarkIris's stated purpose is to "create and promote a broader array of engaging, immersive, and captivating mobile game genres for a global audience" — a generic mission interchangeable with any mobile game developer [3]. The statement does not articulate a deeper purpose or vision beyond the obvious business activity. Community Impact (0/2): No evidence of charitable giving, community programs, or philanthropy was found in any public materials or filings [1][2][3]. Employee Retention (0/2): With only 19 employees and no publicly available retention data, this item cannot be scored. The company is too small for meaningful retention metrics to be publicly reported [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): CEO and Chairman Zhifang Hong participated directly in the April 2026 PIPE financing, purchasing all 1,428,571 Class B ordinary shares at $0.35 per share, demonstrating personal financial commitment to the company's strategic direction [5]. However, this occurred in the context of a deeply discounted private placement, not open-market purchases at prevailing prices.

infrastructure.label: AT RISK
infrastructure.analysis: LIQUIDITY FLOOR TRIGGERED: Average daily volume of 53,386 shares multiplied by the stock price of $4.63 equals approximately $247,177 in daily dollar volume, well below the $1,000,000 minimum threshold required by the IOWN framework [2]. Infrastructure score is automatically set to 0. For reference, the underlying metrics paint a mixed picture: the company carries zero debt (D/E of 0%), which is favorable, but FY2025 produced a net loss of $8.64 million on $10.08 million in revenue, resulting in a deeply negative profit margin of -85.75% and negative ROE of approximately -127% [1]. Forward P/E and PEG ratio are not applicable due to negative earnings. The company's $1.8 million cash position as of September 2025 was supplemented by a $3.8 million PIPE financing in April 2026, but burn rate raises going-concern questions [1][5]. A 1-for-16 reverse stock split was executed May 11, 2026 to maintain NASDAQ listing compliance [6].

ai_resilience.label: HIGH RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (3/9): EXPOSED. DarkIris operates in mobile game development, a sector facing significant AI disruption risk. Generative AI tools are rapidly lowering the cost and barriers to entry for game development — Morgan Stanley estimates AI could cut game development costs in half, creating a $22 billion profit opportunity but also dramatically increasing competition [7]. For a micro-cap developer like DarkIris with no proprietary IP moat, no platform advantage, and no brand recognition, AI-powered tools enable larger studios and individual developers alike to produce comparable content faster and cheaper. The company's core business of developing and publishing mobile games in the Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan markets has no structural defense against AI-enabled competitors flooding the market with lower-cost alternatives. The company's own pivot to AIGC acknowledges this reality — it is attempting to ride the AI wave rather than be submerged by it, but execution risk is extreme for a 19-person company with negative cash flow [1][3]. AI Opportunity (0/1): While DarkIris has announced an AIGC platform initiative and established the AETHER INTELLIGENCE R&D hub in Singapore, the platform remains in pre-commercial development with a Beta 1.0 target of Q3 2026 [4]. No AI-related revenue has been generated and the platform has not been validated commercially.

infinite_game.summary: DarkIris is a young, micro-cap company playing a distinctly finite game. The company's strategic direction shifts with market trends — from mobile gaming to AIGC — suggesting reactive positioning rather than an enduring Just Cause. With only 19 employees, $5.8 million market cap, and a history of significant share dilution, the company is playing to survive, not playing to keep playing. The reverse stock split to maintain NASDAQ compliance, deep operating losses, and reliance on PIPE financing all signal a finite orientation focused on near-term survival rather than long-term capability building.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (0/3): No Just Cause is evident. DarkIris exists to develop and publish mobile games and is now pivoting to AIGC content — these are business strategies, not a vision of a better future. The Chairman's Letter to Shareholders focuses entirely on revenue growth, strategic pivots, and market positioning [3]. There is no articulated purpose beyond making money in whatever segment appears most promising. The rapid pivot from pure mobile gaming to AIGC suggests opportunism rather than an enduring cause.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (0/2): With only 19 employees and no publicly available information about organizational culture, employee satisfaction, turnover, or trust dynamics, this dimension cannot be meaningfully evaluated [2]. The company is too small and too young as a public entity (IPO August 2025) to have established a track record on team trust. The establishment of a separate R&D entity in Singapore introduces geographic fragmentation that could challenge cohesion.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): No evidence of an infinite competitive mindset. The company's public communications focus on market opportunity and growth strategy rather than learning from competitors [3]. There is no acknowledgment of worthy rivals whose strengths reveal DarkIris's own weaknesses. Competitive positioning language is about capturing market share in AIGC, not about self-improvement through competitive awareness.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): The pivot from pure mobile game development to a dual-engine "core gaming + AIGC" strategy represents meaningful strategic evolution [3][4]. Establishing AETHER INTELLIGENCE in Singapore and pursuing a platform model (MaaS/PaaS) for game developers and filmmakers shows willingness to transform the business. However, the pivot appears driven by market necessity and survival instinct (the core gaming business is losing money) rather than by a Just Cause, and the company has not yet demonstrated the ability to execute this transformation.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): CEO Zhifang Hong personally invested in the April 2026 PIPE financing and is betting the company's future on an unproven AIGC platform in a highly competitive space [5]. Establishing a new R&D hub in Singapore while the core business is cash-flow negative takes some courage. However, these decisions may reflect necessity rather than principled long-term thinking — the core mobile gaming business is producing deep losses and the company needed a new narrative to attract capital.

investment_thesis: DarkIris Inc. is a micro-cap mobile game developer headquartered in Hong Kong with FY2025 revenue of $10.08 million (up 27.3% YoY) but a net loss of $8.64 million, resulting in a deeply negative profit margin of -85.75% [1]. The company is attempting a strategic pivot from pure mobile gaming to an AIGC-powered content creation platform spanning gaming and film production, with a Singapore-based R&D hub (AETHER INTELLIGENCE) targeting Beta 1.0 by Q3 2026 [3][4]. CEO Zhifang Hong personally participated in a $3.8 million PIPE financing at $0.35 per share in April 2026, demonstrating personal commitment, but the deeply discounted placement price also reflects significant dilution and market skepticism [5].
thesis_continued: The fundamental challenges are severe. With a $5.8 million market cap, 19 employees, and $1.8 million cash (supplemented by the PIPE), DarkIris is attempting to compete in AI content generation against companies with thousands of engineers and billions in resources. The AIGC platform vision — evolving into a MaaS/PaaS offering for global content creators — is extraordinarily ambitious for a company of this size and financial position. The 1-for-16 reverse stock split in May 2026 to maintain NASDAQ listing compliance, combined with ongoing cash burn and reliance on equity dilution for financing, raises serious going-concern questions [6]. Average daily dollar volume of approximately $247,000 makes the stock essentially untradeable for institutional investors and fails the IOWN liquidity floor, resulting in an Infrastructure score of 0. This is a speculative micro-cap with an unproven pivot strategy, not a suitable investment for the IOWN framework.
key_catalysts: AIGC platform Beta 1.0 launch targeted for Q3 2026 could validate the technology and attract early commercial partnerships with game developers and filmmakers [3][4]
AETHER INTELLIGENCE Singapore R&D hub establishment positions the company in a global AI talent market and could accelerate platform development beyond what the Hong Kong team alone could achieve [4]
Successful commercialization of the MaaS/PaaS platform model could create a recurring SaaS revenue stream with higher margins than the core mobile gaming business [3]
key_risks: Going-concern risk: $8.64 million net loss against $10.08 million revenue with only $1.8 million cash (pre-PIPE) — the company burns more cash than it generates in revenue, and the $3.8 million PIPE provides limited runway [1][5]
AIGC platform execution risk is extreme — 19 employees competing against tech giants with massive AI budgets in a rapidly commoditizing space where open-source AI tools are proliferating
Severe illiquidity ($247K daily dollar volume) and a 1-for-16 reverse stock split to maintain NASDAQ compliance signal a stock at risk of delisting if the share price continues to deteriorate [6]
